title: Dornston: Campsite & St. Nicholas Church

main_route.excerpt: Simon, Eve, Tinder → Dornston. Campsite with Fort Laramie + tilted horse!

stops.normalized_name: St. Nicholas Church, Dornston, Herefordshire, UK
stops.excerpt: We saw St. Nicholas church. V. old buttresses.

boundary_flags: B_0316_L is marked mixed_end=true; the April 5th 2019 entry on the same page belongs to a different trip and has been excluded from this route.
summary: A short two-day camping weekend in late September 2018, with Simon and Eve (and Tinder the dog) driving from Birmingham to the campsite at Dornston in Herefordshire. The visit included a peaceful walk to the very old St. Nicholas church, blackberrying, damsoning, and admiring the beautiful houses and wildlife before returning home after lunch on the Saturday.
extraction_notes: Single diary page (B_0316_L); trip dates inferred as Friday 28 Sept – Saturday 29 Sept 2018 (year confirmed by trip_id and surrounding volume context). Sons are not mentioned as travelling on this trip — only Simon, Eve, and Tinder. The page is boundary_mixed at the end: the 'April 5th 2019' entry beginning 'Simon, Eve + Tinder come to near Barnsley' is a separate trip and excluded. The Alexander Kirby WW1 reference is noted but is contextual commentary, not a place stop. 'Fort Laramie' appears to be a field name or local landmark at the campsite, not a separate stop. Dornston is a small hamlet in Herefordshire; St. Nicholas church is the local parish church there.
